2604.15244.title: From Tokens to Steps: Verification-Aware Speculative Decoding for Efficient Multi-Step Reasoning
2604.15244.paper_summary: Speculative decoding (SD) accelerates large language model inference by allowing a lightweight draft model to propose outputs that a stronger target model verifies. However, its token-centric nature allows erroneous steps to propagate. Prior approaches mitigate this using external reward models, but incur additional latency, computational overhead, and limit generalizability. We propose SpecGuard, a verification-aware speculative decoding framework that performs step-level verification using only model-internal signals. At each step, SpecGuard samples multiple draft candidates and selects the most consistent step, which is then validated using an ensemble of two lightweight model-internal signals: (i) an attention-based grounding score that measures attribution to the input and previously accepted steps, and (ii) a log-probability-based score that captures token-level confidence. These signals jointly determine whether a step is accepted or recomputed using the target, allocating compute selectively. Experiments across a range of reasoning benchmarks show that SpecGuard improves accuracy by 3.6% while reducing latency by ~11%, outperforming both SD and reward-guided SD.
2604.15244.paper_summary_zh: 推測解碼（SD）通過允許輕量級草稿模型提出輸出，讓更強大的目標模型進行驗證，從而加速大型語言模型的推理。然而，其以標記為中心的特性允許錯誤步驟的傳播。先前的方法通過使用外部獎勵模型來減輕這一問題，但會產生額外的延遲、計算開銷，並限制一般化能力。我們提出了SpecGuard，一個具有驗證意識的推測解碼框架，僅使用模型內部信號進行逐步驗證。在每一步中，SpecGuard 會抽樣多個草稿候選者並選擇最一致的一步，然後使用兩個輕量級模型內部信號的集成來進行驗證：（i）一個基於注意力的基準分數，用於衡量對輸入和先前接受步驟的歸因，以及（ii）一個基於對數概率的分數，用於捕捉標記級別的信心。這些信號共同決定某一步是被接受還是使用目標重新計算，並選擇性地分配計算資源。針對一系列推理基準的實驗顯示，SpecGuard 將準確性提高了 3.6%，同時將延遲減少了約 11%，超越了 SD 和獎勵引導的 SD。
2604.15244.author: Kiran Purohit et.al.
2604.15244.authors: Kiran Purohit, Ramasuri Narayanam, Soumyabrata Pal
